methods.description: Process for tagging bees and collecting pollen samples to study foraging behavior and resource collection.

steps.description: Bees were allowed to exit and re-enter the colony throughout the experiment. We undertook individual tagging of workers on the first 2 days after transfer to the field and opportunistically afterwards during the pollen collection process, using coloured, numbered queen-marking tags.
steps.searchable_summary: tag bees with colored, numbered tags for identification

steps.description: We collected pollen from foragers returning to each colony in the afternoon and in the evening, by gently capturing individuals as they entered the tunnels of the external box, but before they entered the colony, and removing the pollen with a toothpick.
steps.searchable_summary: collect pollen from returning forager bees

steps.description: Light level (lx) and temperature (C) were recorded every 30 s throughout the experiment using an automated data logger.
steps.searchable_summary: record light level and temperature every 30 seconds

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected data on bee activity and pollen collection.

steps.description: We used generalized linear mixed models (GLMMs) to assess the difference in colony activity (exit count and return count, separately) between the afternoon and the evening.
steps.searchable_summary: use GLMMs to assess differences in colony activity

steps.description: We analysed the masses of the full pollen samples to explore the effects of time period and light condition on how much pollen the bees returned with.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze pollen sample masses for time and light effects

steps.description: To determine whether foragers acquired the same amount of pollen during the evening as in the day, we modelled pollen load mass by the fixed factor period (afternoon or evening).
steps.searchable_summary: model pollen load mass by time period

steps.description: To investigate the effects of declining light levels on pollen load mass, we modelled the pollen load mass of evening samples by time until sunset and light level at time of return.
steps.searchable_summary: model pollen load mass by time until sunset and light level

steps.description: To assess the masses of pollen from repeat samples, we conducted a paired Wilcoxon signed-rank test.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct paired Wilcoxon signed-rank test on pollen masses

steps.description: Using a one-way ANOVA test, we compared the thorax width of bees in three categories.
steps.searchable_summary: perform one-way ANOVA on bee thorax width

steps.description: We modelled the thorax width of bees returning with samples in the evening as a response variable by the fixed factor 'time until sunset at return'.
steps.searchable_summary: model thorax width by time until sunset

steps.description: To determine whether pollen load mass was related to bee size, we modelled load mass by thorax width in a GLMM.
steps.searchable_summary: model pollen load mass by thorax width

methods.description: Procedures for acquiring and analyzing data on bee activity and pollen samples.

steps.description: During the observation times, we videorecorded bees entering and leaving each nest entrance.
steps.searchable_summary: videorecord bee entry and exit at nest entrance

steps.description: Exit and entry events were extracted automatically using a custom-written code in MatLab.
steps.searchable_summary: extract exit and entry events using MatLab code

steps.description: We froze pollen samples at -20 C until the end of the field work and then transported them to the laboratory for analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: freeze and transport pollen samples for analysis

steps.description: We defrosted each pollen sample before weighing it on precision scales.
Following weighing, we mounted each sample on a microscope slide.
steps.searchable_summary: mount pollen samples on microscope slides

steps.description: We photographed the bee-collected samples and the floral reference collection at various magnifications.
steps.searchable_summary: photograph pollen samples at different magnifications

steps.description: We used these photographs to identify pollen to the lowest possible taxonomic level by its morphological features.
At the end of the experiment, we froze and subsequently dissected the bee colonies, and measured the individuals that had retained their identification tags.
steps.searchable_summary: freeze, dissect, and measure tagged bees
